title: Trigeminal Neuralgia
chief_complaint: I get these electric shock-like pains in my right cheek. It lasts seconds but it's the worst pain I've ever felt. It's triggered by touching my face, brushing my teeth, even a breeze. I'm afraid to eat or talk.

physical_exam.Neurologic: Normal facial sensation to light touch and pinprick in all 3 trigeminal divisions. Normal corneal reflex. No motor weakness. No other cranial nerve deficits. Trigger zones: Right nasolabial fold and upper lip (light touch triggers pain).

hidden_data.mri_brain: Neurovascular compression of right trigeminal nerve root by superior cerebellar artery. No mass. No MS plaques.

correct_diagnosis: Classic Trigeminal Neuralgia (Tic Douloureux) from Neurovascular Compression
key_learning_points: Trigeminal neuralgia = paroxysmal, severe, lancinating facial pain in distribution of CN V. Usually unilateral
Classic TN: Episodic, triggered by innocuous stimuli. Secondary TN: Associated with MS or tumor
Pathophysiology: Neurovascular compression of trigeminal nerve root (usually SCA) → demyelination → ephaptic transmission
Treatment: Carbamazepine 200-400mg daily (first-line, NNT=1.7). Oxcarbazepine alternative. Baclofen or Gabapentin as add-on
Surgical: Microvascular decompression (MVD) if refractory to medical therapy. 90% initial success rate

Differentiate from dental pain (not triggered by touch, no paroxysmal nature), cluster headache (longer duration, autonomic features), and TMJ disorder (worse with jaw movement, tenderness over joint)
